--- title: "Bacteroidota/Baccilota ratio" author: - name: Muluh --- ::: {.cell} ::: ### Group-wise comparisons - **Taxonomic level:** Phylum - **Group variable:** diet - **Multiple testing correction:** fdr - **Statistical test** Wilcoxon In the report, a suffix of 1~ Baseline and 2~ Week 6 ::: {.cell} ::: ::: {.cell} ::: ::: {.cell} ::: ::: {.cell} ::: ::: {.cell} ::: ::: {.cell} ::: ::: {.cell} ::: ::: {.cell} ::: ::: {.cell} ::: ::: {.cell} ::: ::: {.cell} ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](ratio_files/figure-html/fig-figure-of-findings-before-after-1.png){#fig-figure-of-findings-before-after fig-align='center' width=480} ::: ::: #### Table of the top findings #### Across-group ##### Before treatment - Multiple testing correction: **fdr** - Statistical test: **Wilcoxon** ::: {#tbl-table-of-findings-before .cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ##### After treatment - Multiple testing correction: **fdr** - Statistical test: **Wilcoxon** ::: {#tbl-table-of-findings-after .cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](ratio_files/figure-html/fig-figure-of-findings-within-1.png){#fig-figure-of-findings-within fig-align='center' width=480} ::: ::: #### Within-group ##### Paired treatment - Multiple testing correction: **fdr** - Statistical test: **paired Wilcoxon** ::: {#tbl-table-of-findings-paired .cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### LMM Research Question 1: Did the diet change the microbiota? - The interaction term (Treatment:Timepoint) tests whether the effect of time on B/F ratio depends on the diet group. - Post-hoc analysis within each diet group identifies specific changes in B/F ratio over time. Research Question 2: Was the microbiota equal between the diet groups before and after diet? - The main effect of diet tests for differences in B/F ratio between diet groups at baseline. - Post-hoc analysis across diet groups at each timepoint identifies specific differences between diets Interpret the Results - Main Effects: - diet: Tests for differences in microbiota between Diet A and Diet B at Baseline. - duration: Tests for changes in microbiota over time across both diets. - diet:duration: Tests whether the effect of Timepoint depends on the diet group. - Random Effects: - (1|id): Accounts for within-subject variability. - **SE (Standard Error)**: Measures the uncertainty in the estimate. Smaller SE means more confidence in the effect. - **DF (Degrees of Freedom)**: Used to assess statistical significance; depends on the number of observations and model complexity. - **T00**: The variance of the random intercept (or slope) for a grouping factor (subject ID). Reflects variability between groups. - **Nid**: The number of levels in the grouping factor (number of subjects). - **ICC (Intra-Class Correlation)** or `iccand`: The proportion of total variance attributable to the grouping structure. Higher ICC means more similarity within groups. ::: {.cell} ::: - Multiple testing correction: **FDR** - Statistical test: **paired Wilcoxon** - Formula used: **~ diet * timepoint + (1 | id)** ::: {#tbl-table-of-interaction .cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}

Week 6 |rice | -0.2118038| 0.1953264| 67| -1.0843584| 0.2820925| |Baseline - Week 6 |oat | 0.0107015| 0.1981780| 67| 0.0539994| 0.9570964| ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](ratio_files/figure-html/fig-figure-of-interaction-within-1.png){#fig-figure-of-interaction-within width=672} ::: ::: *Across-Group Comparisons* How to read the results; - For Marginal Means (emmeans): - Negative emmean:"The mean log2(B/F ratio) for Diet rice at baseline was `-2.238227` (95% CI: -2.607414 , -1.869040), indicating Firmicutes dominance (B/F ≈ 0.213)." - Positive emmean:"if the mean log2(B/F ratio) for Diet oat was `0.4` (95% CI: 0.2, 0.6), then Bacteroidetes dominance (B/F ≈ 1.32)." - For Contrasts: - Negative estimate:"Diet oat had a significantly lower log2(B/F ratio) than diet rice at timepoint 1 (estimate = `-1.0272585`, borderline p < 0.05), reflecting greater Firmicutes dominance." - Positive estimate:"Diet oat had a higher log2(B/F ratio) than diet rice at timepoint 2 (estimate = `0.5024736 `, p = 0.51), reflecting a non significant Bacteroidetes dominance." ::: {#tbl-table-of-interaction-across-emmeans .cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} Table: Estimated Marginal Means for Diets |diet |timepoint | coef| 2 ^ coef| SE| df| lower.CL| lower.CL_actual| upper.CL| upper.CL_actual| |:----|:---------|---------:|---------:|---------:|--------:|---------:|---------------:|---------:|---------------:| |rice |Baseline | -2.264921| 0.2080611| 0.1896295| 109.7968| -2.640730| 0.1603471| -1.889112| 0.2699732| |oat |Baseline | -1.976809| 0.2540511| 0.1923980| 109.7968| -2.358105| 0.1950472| -1.595514| 0.3309043| |rice |Week 6 | -2.053117| 0.2409629| 0.1896295| 109.7968| -2.428926| 0.1857036| -1.677308| 0.3126655| |oat |Week 6 | -1.987511| 0.2521736| 0.1923980| 109.7968| -2.368806| 0.1936057| -1.606215| 0.3284589| ::: ::: ::: {#tbl-table-of-interaction-across-contrasts .cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} Table: Estimated contrasts for Diets |contrast |duration | estimate| SE| df| t.ratio| p.value| |:----------|:--------|----------:|---------:|--------:|----------:|---------:| |rice -
